directions.name: Activate The Dormant 128x128x32 Hot-Band Branch
directions.hypothesis: No new explicit human-review idea family is queued in state/human_review.md, so the ranking is driven by the measured run itself. The latest hybrid prefetch permutation improved runtime from 25.62406445 ms to 25.47660828 ms and cut the dominant hot-band kernel's barrier stall from 6.63 to 5.52 while trimming registers from 202 to 200, but the same hot-band launch still dominates at 32.97 ms with only 16.54% active warps, 47.72% tensor activity, and a worse 8.02% long-scoreboard stall. That means the prefetch-order family is likely exhausted. The next best move is to change staging granularity, not another A/B ordering tweak: switch the hot band to the existing 128x128x32 kernel so each stage covers 32 K values and halves the steady-state handoff frequency on the same 128-thread CTA shape.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Synchronization and latency-hiding limits in the current 128x128 K16 hot-band cadence, especially the two-block occupancy ceiling and rising long-scoreboard stalls.
directions.risk: Medium-high. This is a structural hot-band branch and earlier K32 work carried correctness risk, but the kernel already exists in the current source and it is the highest-upside family that directly attacks the measured occupancy and handoff problem.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the K32 branch breaks correctness, or if it does not beat 25.47660828 ms while also improving the hot-band barrier and scoreboard profile.

directions.name: Use The Existing 128x128 Two-Stage Sibling As The Lower-Risk Control
directions.hypothesis: If the K32 branch is too invasive for the next loop, the cleanest control is the existing two-stage 128x128 sibling. It keeps the same hot-band CTA and 128-thread launch shape as the current PTX microkernel, but it swaps to the simpler sibling consume/store path and refill cadence. That makes it the best way to test whether the remaining loss is tied to the current microkernel-specific helper path and its 200-register footprint rather than to the 128x128 launch geometry itself.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register pressure and helper-path latency inside the current hot-band PTX microkernel rather than raw DRAM bandwidth.
directions.risk: Medium. This is a narrower change than the K32 branch, but it is still only a control path and should be treated as secondary because similar sibling experiments previously reopened DRAM pressure when they drifted too far from the current microkernel path.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if this sibling path reopens the old high-DRAM loss pattern or stays slower than the current 25.47660828 ms control.

directions.name: Restore The 6144 Hot-Band/Peeled Seam Only As An Audit Fallback
directions.hypothesis: The round-18 autotune files still show that broader 64x384-style fixed-main controls once mattered, but the current dedicated hot-band path already lives in a different regime and broad dispatch reopening is not the next tradeoff. The last explicitly measured bounded fallback is the 6144 seam variant, which previously recovered to 25.65470409 ms with DRAM held near 11.25 and long-scoreboard near 5.91. The latest hybrid prefetch run already beats that number, so the seam family should now be kept only as an audit fallback to test whether the residual loss sits at the hot-band/peeled boundary rather than inside the dominant hot-band kernel itself.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Boundary cost between the dominant 128x128 PTX hot-band launch and the peeled 64x384 follow-on band, not the hot-band inner microkernel.
directions.risk: Medium. The family is measured and bounded, but its known local best is already slower than the current run, so it should stay tertiary and should not reopen a deeper seam line search.
directions.stop_condition: Do not go deeper than the known 6144 pivot; stop immediately if it remains slower than 25.47660828 ms or simply shifts work into the peeled kernel.

notes: Round 1/50 diagnosis for run 20260420_164612_bf16_gemm_v1_da1a5bb. No new explicit human-review idea family is queued in state/human_review.md. The latest implemented hybrid prefetch ordering improved runtime from 25.62406445 ms to 25.47660828 ms and cut hot-band barrier pressure from 6.63 to 5.52 while reducing registers from 202 to 200, but the dominant 128x128 PTX hot-band kernel still accounts for 32.97 ms, remains capped at two resident blocks with only 16.54% active warps, and traded that barrier win for worse long-scoreboard stall (5.60 to 8.02). That closes the prefetch-order family as the next primary move. The round-18 fixed-main autotune sweep is still useful as historical evidence that broader tile-width changes can matter, but on the current dedicated hot-band path the next step should stay inside the hot-band kernel family: first a staged 128x128x32 branch, then the simpler two-stage sibling as a control, with the previously measured 6144 seam held only as a tertiary audit fallback.
